descriptionShort: Humboldt Broncos defeat Richmond Sockeyes 5-3 in overtime in opening game of Centennial Cup Western Canada final.
descriptionLong: The Humboldt Broncos scored twice in overtime to beat the Richmond Sockeyes 5-3 on April 21, 1987, in the opening game of the best-of-seven Centennial Cup Western Canada junior hockey final. Curtis Chamberlain scored three goals including the winner, while Brad Bergen and Bill McDougall added markers for Saskatchewan's champions. Richmond's goals came from Bryan Moller, Matt Hervey, and Jason Phillips. The Sockeyes led 2-1 after two periods before the teams tied 3-3 at regulation.
